name: EOQ Inventory Calculator
description: Industrial-grade Economic Order Quantity calculator with integrated cost analysis, safety stock, and reorder point optimization. Compliant with ISO 9001, Lean, Six Sigma, and WERC standards.

inputs.label: Annual Demand (units)
inputs.businessContext: Total forecasted demand for the item over one year. Used to calculate order frequency and total cycle stock.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total forecasted demand for the item over one year. Used to calculate order frequency and total cycle stock.

inputs.label: Ordering Cost per Order
inputs.businessContext: Fixed cost incurred per purchase order (e.g., administrative, inspection, transportation setup).

businessContext_i18n.en: Fixed cost incurred per purchase order (e.g., administrative, inspection, transportation setup).

inputs.label: Holding Cost per Unit per Year
inputs.businessContext: Cost to hold one unit in inventory for one year, including storage, insurance, obsolescence, and capital cost.

label_i18n.en: Holding Cost per Unit per Year

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost to hold one unit in inventory for one year, including storage, insurance, obsolescence, and capital cost.

inputs.label: Unit Cost (Purchase Price)
inputs.businessContext: Purchase price per unit from supplier. Used to calculate total annual purchase cost and capital tied up.

businessContext_i18n.en: Purchase price per unit from supplier. Used to calculate total annual purchase cost and capital tied up.

inputs.label: Lead Time (days)
inputs.businessContext: Average time from order placement to receipt. Critical for safety stock and reorder point calculations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average time from order placement to receipt. Critical for safety stock and reorder point calculations.

inputs.label: Demand Variability (Std Dev of Daily Demand)
inputs.businessContext: Standard deviation of daily demand. Used to calculate safety stock under uncertainty (Six Sigma approach).

label_i18n.en: Demand Variability (Std Dev of Daily Demand)

businessContext_i18n.en: Standard deviation of daily demand. Used to calculate safety stock under uncertainty (Six Sigma approach).

inputs.label: Desired Service Level
inputs.businessContext: Target probability of not stocking out during lead time. Higher service levels increase safety stock.

businessContext_i18n.en: Target probability of not stocking out during lead time. Higher service levels increase safety stock.

inputs.label: Backorder Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Cost incurred per unit when demand cannot be met from stock (lost sales, penalty, expediting).

businessContext_i18n.en: Cost incurred per unit when demand cannot be met from stock (lost sales, penalty, expediting).

rules.message: Annual demand must be greater than zero.
Ordering cost, holding cost, and unit cost must be positive.
Service level must be one of the predefined values.
EOQ exceeds warehouse capacity. Consider reducing order quantity or increasing capacity.

thresholds.message: Total inventory cost exceeds 20% of total purchase cost. Review holding or ordering costs.
Safety stock is more than 50% of EOQ. High demand variability or service level may be driving inefficiency.
Inventory turns are below 4. Consider reducing order quantity or increasing demand.
Space cost is more than 30% of total holding cost. Evaluate warehouse layout or item footprint.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
